children.heading: Use of sustainable development assistance to support further economic growth
children.label: a Use of sustainable development assistance to support further economic growth
children.text: It is the sense of the Congress that sustained economic growth in sub-Saharan Africa depends in large measure upon the development of a receptive environment for trade and investment, and that to achieve this objective the United States Agency for International Development should continue to support programs which help to create this environment. Investments in human resources, development, and implementation of free market policies, including policies to liberalize agricultural markets and improve food security, and the support for the rule of law and democratic governance should continue to be encouraged and enhanced on a bilateral and regional basis.

The Development Fund for Africa established under chapter 10 of part I of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.) has been an effective tool in providing development assistance to sub-Saharan Africa since 1988.22 U.S.C. 2293

The Development Fund for Africa will complement the other provisions of this chapter and lay a foundation for increased trade and investment opportunities between the United States and sub-Saharan Africa.

children.label: A
children.text: Strengthening primary and vocational education systems, especially the acquisition of middle-level technical skills for operating modern private businesses and the introduction of college level business education, including the study of international business, finance, and stock exchanges.

children.label: C
children.text: Supporting democratization, good governance and civil society and conflict resolution efforts.

children.label: D
children.text: Increasing food security by promoting the expansion of agricultural and agriculture-based industrial production and productivity and increasing real incomes for poor individuals.

children.label: E
children.text: Promoting an enabling environment for private sector-led growth through sustained economic reform, privatization programs, and market-led economic activities.

children.label: F
children.text: Promoting decentralization and local participation in the development process, especially linking the rural production sectors and the industrial and market centers throughout Africa.

children.label: G
children.text: Increasing the technical and managerial capacity of sub-Saharan African individuals to manage the economy of sub-Saharan Africa.

children.label: H
children.text: Ensuring sustainable economic growth through environmental protection.

The African Development Foundation has a unique congressional mandate to empower the poor to participate fully in development and to increase opportunities for gainful employment, poverty alleviation, and more equitable income distribution in sub-Saharan Africa. The African Development Foundation has worked successfully to enhance the role of women as agents of change, strengthen the informal sector with an emphasis on supporting micro and small sized enterprises, indigenous technologies, and mobilizing local financing. The African Development Foundation should develop and implement strategies for promoting participation in the socioeconomic development process of grassroots and informal sector groups such as nongovernmental organizations, cooperatives, artisans, and traders into the programs and initiatives established under this chapter.
